tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Afghan Refugee Becomes First Woman from Her Country to Summit Mount Everest
stories.summary: Afghan refugee River Ahmad became the first woman from her country to summit Mount Everest in May, aiming to raise $200,000 for an online school for girls denied education by the Taliban.

stories.headline: Displaced Grandmother Bakes Daily for Thousands of War Refugees
stories.summary: Soubhiye Zeiter, 63, fled her Beirut home with 15 family members and now bakes mana'eesh daily outside her tent, greeting dozens of customers each morning with coffee and warmth.

stories.headline: 40-Year-Old Cape Verde Goalkeeper Delivers Magical Shutout in Historic World Cup Debut
stories.summary: Vozinha, 40, made his World Cup debut holding Spain scoreless with seven saves, moved to tears thinking of his grandparents who raised him and his mother unable to afford a U.S. visa.

stories.headline: Late-Blooming Goaltender Brandon Bussi Backstops Hurricanes to First Stanley Cup in 20 Years
stories.summary: Journey-man Brandon Bussi stepped in and led Carolina to their first championship, proving dreams can bloom at any moment.

stories.headline: Man with ALS Speaks and Works Through Breakthrough Brain Implant
stories.summary: Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, has used his brain implant over 3,800 hours independently to work, email, and read to his young daughter.

stories.headline: 'Batman of Whakatāne': Stranger Drives Elderly Man Home After Hospital Mix-Up
stories.summary: Richard Hayes, mistakenly called to pick up a stranger from the hospital, drove 86-year-old Norman Camburn home anyway, refusing payment because "everywhere's 15 minutes in Whakatāne."

stories.headline: South Boston Community Rallies Around Children Robbed at Lemonade Stand
stories.summary: Hundreds of South Boston neighbors packed the Lower End Lemonade Sale organized by Nicole Raukohl to support the Byrne children robbed at gunpoint, turning trauma into community triumph.

stories.headline: Alabama Firefighter Finishes Patient's Lawn After Medical Emergency
stories.summary: Lieutenant Colton Brown stayed after a medical emergency call to mow an elderly resident's lawn, easing their burden with quiet, practical compassion.

stories.headline: One-Time CRISPR Treatment Cuts Hereditary Swelling Attacks by 87%
stories.summary: Eighty patients with hereditary angioedema receiving a single CRISPR gene-edit saw dangerous attacks plummet by 87%, with 62% remaining attack-free without ongoing medication.

stories.headline: Brain-Reading Pacemaker Helps Parkinson's Patients Walk Steadier
stories.summary: UC San Francisco researchers created an adaptive deep brain stimulation implant that reads each step and adjusts therapy within fractions of a second, helping Parkinson's patients walk steadier.

stories.headline: Rare Red-Headed Woodpeckers Return to Ontario's Restored Tallgrass Prairie
stories.summary: After more than two decades of restoration work, endangered Red-headed Woodpeckers have joyfully returned to Ontario's Rice Lake Plains tallgrass prairie.

stories.headline: Spain Powers National Grid Entirely on Pure Hydrogen in World First
stories.summary: Wärtsilä's groundbreaking engine proves fossil-fuel-free renewable energy storage is viable at scale.
